content.boundary: Applies to village primary schools (grades 1-4/1-6) below a to-be-defined minimum enrollment/viability threshold in settlements under 2,000 inhabitants; excludes schools above threshold and does not apply to secondary education.
content.change_lever: A codified consolidation procedure activated when a school falls below a transparent minimum-viability threshold, coupled with a statutory (not disputed) transport-guarantee obligation, published cost/enrollment data, and TEIP-style targeted support funding for displaced and disadvantaged pupils at the receiving school.
content.name: Consolidation-with-mitigation: default closure paired with statutory transport, funding transparency, and targeted equity support
content.system_problem: Current closures occur via ad hoc administrative decisions citing efficiency/enrollment ratios, with disputed legal responsibility for pupil transport, no dedicated statutory closure procedure, and no systematic targeted support for displaced pupils, meaning consolidation happens without the compensatory infrastructure that would offset known harms (long commutes, segregation, disrupted achievement).
